title: Podman doesn`t like IPv6 being turned off in the kernel
body:
/kind bug

**Description**

When i turn off IPv6 podman has issues. It looks like it doesn`t know if it can use IPv6 or not.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Change grub.cfg and add ipv6.disable=1
2. reboot
3. Use the gitlab runner to pull images over the API

Unfortunatelly I have not figured out how to reproduce it without the gitlab runner.

**Describe the results you received:**

This is just the output from the gitlab runner using the podman API. I do not know  how to reproduce it just with podman.

```
Preparing the "docker" executor
04:46
Using Docker executor with image registry.gitlab.com/xrow-public/ci-tools/tools:3.0 ...
Authenticating with credentials from job payload (GitLab Registry)
Pulling docker image registry.gitlab.com/xrow-public/ci-tools/tools:3.0 ...
WARNING: Failed to pull image with policy "always": Cannot connect to the Docker daemon at unix:///var/run/docker.sock. Is the docker daemon running? (docker.go:147:120s)
ERROR: Preparation failed: failed to pull image "registry.gitlab.com/xrow-public/ci-tools/tools:3.0" with specified policies [always]: Cannot connect to the Docker daemon at unix:///var/run/docker.sock. Is the docker daemon running? (docker.go:147:120s)
Will be retried in 3s ...
Using Docker executor with image registry.gitlab.com/xrow-public/ci-tools/tools:3.0 ...
Authenticating with credentials from job payload (GitLab Registry)
Pulling docker image registry.gitlab.com/xrow-public/ci-tools/tools:3.0 ...
WARNING: Failed to pull image with policy "always": Error response from daemon: Error initializing source docker://registry.gitlab.com/xrow-public/ci-tools/tools:3.0: Get "https://gitlab.com/jwt/auth?account=gitlab-ci-token&scope=repository%3Axrow-public%2Fci-tools%2Ftools%3Apull&service=container_registry": dial tcp [2606:4700:90:0:f22e:fbec:5bed:a9b9]:443: socket: address family not supported by protocol (docker.go:147:23s)
ERROR: Preparation failed: failed to pull image "registry.gitlab.com/xrow-public/ci-tools/tools:3.0" with specified policies [always]: Error response from daemon: Error initializing source docker://registry.gitlab.com/xrow-public/ci-tools/tools:3.0: Get "https://gitlab.com/jwt/auth?account=gitlab-ci-token&scope=repository%3Axrow-public%2Fci-tools%2Ftools%3Apull&service=container_registry": dial tcp [2606:4700:90:0:f22e:fbec:5bed:a9b9]:443: socket: address family not supported by protocol (docker.go:147:23s)
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

Of course no error LOL. Though I think podman should know that IPv6 is disabled and not use it.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
[root@server005 ~]# podman version
Version:      3.0.1
API Version:  3.0.0
Go Version:   go1.15.7
Built:        Fri Feb 19 21:39:38 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

comments.body: @vrothberg PTAL - I'd bet this is c/image.

We're not doing anything specific wrt. ipv4/ipv6.  The errors shows that resolving the host to an address doesn't work, which is outside of our control and depends on how Go resolves.  Can you  check `/etc/resolv.conf` for anything suspicious?

looks good 

```
[root@server005 ~]# cat /etc/resolv.conf
# Generated by NetworkManager
search dc02.xrow.net
nameserver 8.8.8.8
nameserver 4.4.4.4
```

If you think this is not podman related please close the ticket.

Could it be that `search dc02.xrow.net` is interfering?

> If you think this is not podman related please close the ticket.

I am fairly confident it's not a Podman issue.  Can you try pulling from another registry? `podman pull registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora-minimal`?
If you are sure please close.
